title: AlphaFold Sovereign MCP: a Model Context Protocol server for AlphaFold DB and eight other biomedical data sources
description:
AlphaFold Sovereign MCP is an open-source Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that exposes the AlphaFold Protein Structure Database together with eight other public biomedical data sources - Open Targets, ChEMBL, Ensembl VEP, ClinVar, gnomAD, MONDO, HPO, and DisGeNET - as read-only tool calls that a Claude (or other MCP-capable) client can invoke directly.

Tool results can be persisted to a local SQLite knowledge graph through the storage API; the exposed MCP tools query and export that store read-only (automatic per-invocation persistence is not yet wired). The server is engineering-validated with 100% line and branch coverage in continuous integration, but its scientific outputs - draft ACMG/AMP variant evidence and a heuristic druggability tier - have NOT been reviewed by independent domain experts and must be treated as research aids, not as clinical decision support. See LIMITATIONS.md.

The PyPI distribution, the installed console script, and the MCP server identity are all alphafold-sovereign-mcp. The package is Apache-2.0 licensed.
references: Jumper, J. et al. Highly accurate protein structure prediction with AlphaFold. Nature 596, 583-589 (2021). doi:10.1038/s41586-021-03819-2
notes: Engineering-validated with 100% line and branch coverage in CI. Scientific outputs - draft ACMG/AMP variant evidence and a heuristic druggability tier - have NOT been reviewed by independent domain experts and must be treated as research aids, not clinical decision support. See LIMITATIONS.md and STATUS.md in the repository.
